testcases.input: nums = [3,1,-2,-5,2,-4] Output: [3,-2,1,-5,2,-4] Explanation: The positive integers in nums are [3,1,2]. The negative integers are [-2,-5,-4]. The only possible way to rearrange them such that they satisfy all conditions is [3,-2,1,-5,2,-4]. Other ways such as [1,-2,2,-5,3,-4], [3,1,2,-2,-5,-4], [-2,3,-5,1,-4,2] are incorrect because they do not satisfy one or more conditions.
testcases.expectedOutput: [3,-2,1,-5,2,-4] Explanation: The positive integers in nums are [3,1,2]. The negative integers are [-2,-5,-4]. The only possible way to rearrange them such that they satisfy all conditions is [3,-2,1,-5,2,-4]. Other ways such as [1,-2,2,-5,3,-4], [3,1,2,-2,-5,-4], [-2,3,-5,1,-4,2] are incorrect because they do not satisfy one or more conditions.

testcases.input: nums = [-1,1] Output: [1,-1] Explanation: 1 is the only positive integer and -1 the only negative integer in nums. So nums is rearranged to [1,-1].
